goal: Build a real-time crosswalk safety system that detects pedestrians and crosswalk areas, classifies pedestrian state with spatial reasoning, and triggers a Raspberry Pi warning output.

pipeline_summary.name: Data collection
pipeline_summary.description: Collect road and crosswalk videos containing crossing pedestrians, waiting pedestrians, no-risk pedestrians, varied lighting, vehicles, and occlusion.

pipeline_summary.name: Frame extraction
pipeline_summary.description: Videos were sampled into image frames. The predesign recommends one frame every 30 frames, followed by duplicate and blur removal.

pipeline_summary.name: Annotation
pipeline_summary.description: Current dataset is labeled with person rectangles and crosswalk polygons/quadrilaterals in X-AnyLabeling/LabelMe JSON format.

pipeline_summary.name: Label validation
pipeline_summary.description: Validation found complete image/JSON pairing but 15 schema or geometry issues.

pipeline_summary.name: YOLO-Seg dataset conversion
pipeline_summary.description: convert_xanylabeling_to_yoloseg.py converts JSON -> YOLO-Seg at dataset/yolo_seg/ (269 imgs, 3896 person + 945 crosswalk, coords clamped). val currently = train (no split yet).

pipeline_summary.name: YOLO-Seg training and testing
pipeline_summary.description: train_yoloseg.py runs end-to-end. yolo11m-seg, 120 epochs, imgsz 896 -> runs/seg/m_seg_e120/weights/best.pt. Metrics are optimistic (val=train): crosswalk mask mAP50 0.986, person mask mAP50 0.869. Needs a real train/val/test split for honest evaluation.

pipeline_summary.name: Spatial reasoning
pipeline_summary.description: spatial_reasoning.py classifies crossing/waiting/no_risk via perspective-aware geometry: foot-to-crosswalk distance normalized by box height, a min-size gate, and an inner-edge intersection crossing zone. Multi-agent visual audit: 6 good / 2 acceptable / 0 bad, 0 missed crossings. Optional cleanup: small hull erosion + crossing_k fallback review.

pipeline_summary.name: Raspberry Pi deployment
pipeline_summary.description: Run camera capture, model inference, state classification, and warning-light control in real time.

current_dataset.format: X-AnyLabeling / LabelMe JSON paired with JPG images

auto_label_person.py.purpose: Pre-label person rectangles using a pretrained YOLO model and write X-AnyLabeling/LabelMe-compatible JSON files.
auto_label_person.py.default_behavior: Skips images with existing JSON files unless --overwrite is used.

validate_labels.py.purpose: Validate JSON schema, image pairing, image dimensions, allowed labels, shape points, and out-of-bounds coordinates.

yolo11m.pt.purpose: Pretrained YOLO model used for person pre-labeling.

X-AnyLabeling.purpose: Annotation tool used to review person boxes and draw crosswalk polygons.

completed_so_far: Created the project predesign pipeline in CrossguardVision_predesign.pptx.
Extracted representative image frames from source videos.
Used pretrained YOLO-based tooling to pre-label person boxes.
Manually labeled or corrected person and crosswalk annotations in X-AnyLabeling.
Consolidated the current labeled dataset into Crossguard_data.
Normalized dataset names to the crossguard_#### pattern and kept rename_mapping.csv.
Created this README and JSON project progress log.
next_actions: DEFERRED until dataset fully collected: enhance dataset diversity (currently only 2 source clips) and increase crosswalk-area prediction accuracy.
Collect/label new intersection scenes; consider a model-assisted crosswalk pre-labeler using best.pt (mirrors auto_label_person.py).
Create a real train/validation/test split (hold IMG_0232 as test) and retrain for the first HONEST crosswalk mAP (val currently = train).
Optional spatial-reasoning polish: small --hull-erode-frac curb cleanup; review the crossing_k distance fallback (one non-zone false crossing).
Later: integrate Raspberry Pi GPIO warning-light control.
See logs/worklog_2026-06-19.md for the full session record.
notes: The YOLO classes should remain person and crosswalk.
The states crossing, waiting, and no_risk should be produced by spatial reasoning after model inference.
The crosswalk area should be trained as segmentation/mask data because a rectangular crosswalk box is not accurate enough for the downstream safety logic.
